d: To accept or admit that something is true
e: "He acknowledged that he had made a mistake."

d: Having more than one possible meaning; unclear
e: "His answer was ambiguous — we couldn't tell if he agreed."

d: Able to express ideas clearly and effectively
Logical and consistent; easy to understand as a whole
e: "Please give a coherent explanation of what happened."

d: Very interesting or persuasive; impossible to ignore
e: "The documentary made a compelling case for change."

d: To think carefully and deeply about something
e: "She contemplated her career options for weeks."

d: To say the opposite of what someone else has said
To make or become smaller or less; to reduce in importance
e: "His enthusiasm did not diminish over time."

d: To add more detail and explanation to something
To unexpectedly meet or experience something
e: "She encountered many obstacles along the way."

d: To make a process easier or more possible
Basic and most important; essential to the whole
e: "Trust is fundamental to any healthy relationship."

d: Certain to happen and impossible to avoid
Dealing with things in a sensible, practical way
e: "We need a pragmatic solution, not an idealistic one."

d: To strengthen or support something; to confirm
Important enough to have a noticeable effect
e: "There has been a significant improvement in sales."

d: Large in size, value, or importance
e: "She made a substantial contribution to the project."

d: Coming after or following something else
e: "The initial meeting and subsequent follow-ups went well."

d: Present beneath the surface; being the real cause
e: "The underlying cause of the conflict was distrust."

d: To prove that something is correct or true; to make official
To value; to be grateful for
Something accepted as true without proof
A condition or fact affecting a situation
To keep in good condition; to keep going
A likelihood to behave a certain way
